annotations.label: Elliptic integral (classical)

anchor.section: (Lead)
anchor.snippet: an elliptic integral is one of a number of related functions defined as the value of certain integrals

annotations.note: No elliptic-integral definition exists in Mathlib; a grep for `EllipticIntegral` returns nothing.

annotations.label: Elliptic integral (modern)

anchor.section: (Lead)
anchor.snippet: Modern mathematics defines an "elliptic integral" as any function f which can be expressed in the form

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the general R(t, √P(t))-integral form.

annotations.label: Reduction to Legendre canonical forms

anchor.section: (Lead)
anchor.snippet: with the appropriate reduction formula , every elliptic integral can be brought into a form that involves integrals over rational functions and the three Legendre canonical forms

annotations.note: Legendre canonical forms and the reduction theorem are absent from Mathlib.
